architecture_domain.notes: Strong shared recording and tool primitives with coherent relationships, weakened by monolithic coupling and missing hierarchy/cross-bucket invariants.
evidence: submission/server.js:224-293 centralizes recording identity, lifecycle, visibility, creator, bucket, and parent state.
submission/server.js:363-532 centralizes type paths and shared parent, bucket, comment, subscription, and boost projections.
Live project trash left child messages reachable, and submission/server.js:2535-2549 permits card moves without a same-bucket/table check.

endpoint_coverage.notes: All 203 OpenAPI operations are registered and reachable with their specified methods and concrete paths.
evidence: Static extraction counted 131 OpenAPI paths with 203 operations and 204 unique server route registrations.
Parameter-normalized comparison found no missing specified method/path; submission/server.js:3490-3493 correctly handles the embedded .json progress-report parameter.
submission/server.js:1222-1227 adds one extra attachment-download route.

behavioral_depth.notes: Broad CRUD and lifecycle mutations persist in process, but parent lifecycle, bucket invariants, durability, and several partial subsystems are incomplete.
evidence: Live project create/get/update/list/delete and message/comment/archive/reactivate flows persisted expected state and statuses.
Live todo create/complete/uncomplete persisted, and reset removed created state and invalidated old tokens.
Trashing a project did not hide or cascade to its message board children; template construction and several personal/webhook surfaces are shallow.

http_contract.notes: Core shapes and success statuses broadly conform, with material pagination, query, nullable-schema, and URL fidelity defects.
evidence: All 203 implemented operations expose a success status matching a declared OpenAPI 2xx response.
Only 26 of 44 OpenAPI-paginated operations call paginate; examples missing it are submission/server.js:1538-1550, :1931-1934, :2794-2801, and :3811-3824.
submission/server.js:406-410 and :1410-1423 emit .json individual-resource URLs that do not match registered canonical routes; following a returned project URL produced 400.
Runtime schema checks found invalid null string fields in seeded todos, cards, uploads, and account logo output.

seed_fidelity.notes: The required account, cast, project, tools, and rich linked content exist, with several exact inventory and determinism mismatches.
evidence: Runtime seed contained one account, 10 people, the exact eight-person sample cast, one all-access sample project, six required dock tools, and 85 recordings.
Seed counts included five messages, two todo lists/10 todos, 13 cards, one document/two uploads, two schedule entries, and 16 chat lines.
The required analytics subtask, second triage step, on-hold card placement, and Docs row color are absent; chat uses eight people rather than five.
submission/server.js:214-217 leaves the global ID counter unchanged on reset, so reset IDs drift.

request_hardening.notes: Authentication, parsing, validation, limits, and safe errors work, but default credential exposure and verified client project mutation are critical failures.
evidence: Live malformed auth/JSON/IDs/fields/methods returned 401/400/422/405; body and rate limits returned 400 and 429 with Retry-After.
A client bearer could rename and DELETE the sample project because submission/server.js:1481-1499 requires access but no employee/admin capability.
submission/server.js:39 and :744-755 enable unauthenticated raw-token disclosure and full reset by default; startup logs raw tokens at :4284-4298.
A JSON project create sent as text/plain succeeded with 201, demonstrating absent media-type enforcement.

operability.notes: Dependency-free startup, health, configuration, logs, request IDs, CORS, limits, reset, and shutdown are present, with unsafe defaults and no durable/test packaging.
evidence: submission/server.js passes Node syntax checking and uses only built-in modules.
submission/server.js:25-69, :653-809, and :4278-4323 implement environment config, structured logs, health, request IDs, CORS, rate limiting, and graceful signals.
Live handler probes verified health/no-store, configured CORS, rate limits, reset isolation, ALLOW_RESET=false, and shutdown callbacks.
State is process-memory only, no test/conformance package is supplied, and debug credentials/reset are enabled by default.

code_quality.notes: Readable helpers and shared abstractions support a large surface, but the single mutable 4,325-line module has mixed concerns, repeated scans, and fragile invariants.
evidence: Validation, authentication, projections, route registration, recording envelopes, and tool factories are clearly named and reused.
The entire delta is submission/server.js, combining transport, storage, authorization, domain logic, serialization, seed fixtures, and bootstrap.
Repeated full-map scans and duplicated handler mutations contribute to pagination, URL, null-shape, and seed comment/behavior drift.

scope_honesty.notes: Some limitations are disclosed, but production/full-contract claims and successful empty or fabricated-complete partial surfaces are misleading.
evidence: submission/server.js:9-10 discloses process-lifetime storage and :4292-4295 warns when debug endpoints are enabled.
submission/server.js:5 and :736-742 claim a production server and full OpenAPI contract despite material fidelity and security gaps.
The unused 501 helper at submission/server.js:114 contrasts with successful empty question-reminder/readings responses and shallow webhook/template behavior.

summary: A very broad, genuinely stateful in-process implementation with complete declared route coverage and a strong shared recording model. Material deductions come from critical default credential/reset exposure, client-authorized project mutation, non-cascading lifecycle invariants, incomplete pagination and query behavior, schema-invalid nulls, and emitted resource URLs that do not match registered paths.
